final_text:
In the Ellingham diagram, carbon reduces an oxide if the C-CO line lies below the metal-oxide line.  
- At temperatures above \(T_1\), the C-CO line is below the M-MO line, so carbon can reduce MO.  
- At temperatures below \(T_2\), the C-CO line is still above the M′-M′O line, so carbon cannot reduce M′O.  

Thus, only MO is reduced.

[ANSWER]A[/ANSWER]

[ANSWER]A[/ANSWER]
